briefdescription.para: Process once only e.g. take a snap-shot of the scene. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Process every frame. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Enumeration for the refresh-rate of the RenderTask. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Typedef for signals sent by this class. @SINCE_1_0.0.
A pointer to a function for converting screen to frame-buffer coordinates. @SINCE_1_0.0.

parameterdescription.para: The screen coordinates to convert where (0,0) is the top-left of the screen

simplesect.para: True if the conversion was successful, otherwise coordinates should be unmodified

briefdescription.para: A pointer to a function for converting screen to frame-buffer coordinates. @SINCE_1_0.0.

parameterdescription.para: The screen coordinates to convert where (0,0) is the top-left of the screen

simplesect.para: True if the conversion was successful, otherwise coordinates should be unmodified

briefdescription.para: The default conversion function returns false for any screen coordinates.

detaileddescription.para: This effectively disables hit-testing for RenderTasks rendering to a frame buffer. See also FULLSCREEN_FRAMEBUFFER_FUNCTION below.

briefdescription.para: This conversion function outputs the (unmodified) screen coordinates as frame-buffer coordinates.

detaileddescription.para: Therefore the contents of an off-screen image is expected to be rendered "full screen".

briefdescription.para: false
true
Color::BLACK.
false
true
REFRESH_ALWAYS.
Creates an empty RenderTask handle.

detaileddescription.para: This can be initialised with RenderTaskList::CreateRenderTask(). @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Destructor.

detaileddescription.para: This is non-virtual since derived Handle types must not contain data or virtual methods. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: This copy constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.
This assignment operator is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
rhs
A reference to the copied handle
A reference to this

briefdescription.para: Move constructor.
Move assignment operator.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_9.22
rhs
A reference to the moved handle
A reference to this

briefdescription.para: Sets the actors to be rendered. @SINCE_1_0.0.

parameterdescription.para: This actor and its children will be rendered. If actor is an empty handle, then nothing will be rendered

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the actors to be rendered. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: This actor and its children will be rendered

briefdescription.para: Retrives stopper actor. @SINCE_2_3.23.

simplesect.para: The actor that marks where to stop rendering.

briefdescription.para: Sets whether the RenderTask has exclusive access to the source actors; the default is false. @SINCE_1_0.0.

parameterdescription.para: True if the source actors will only be rendered by this render-task

briefdescription.para: Queries whether the RenderTask has exclusive access to the source actors. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: True if the source actors will only be rendered by this render-task

briefdescription.para: Sets whether the render-task should be considered for input handling; the default is true.

detaileddescription.para:
The task used for input handling will be last task in the RenderTaskList which has input enabled, and has a valid source & camera actor. A RenderTask targetting a frame-buffer can still be hit-tested, provided that the screen->frame-buffer coordinate conversion is successful; see also SetScreenToFrameBufferFunction(). @SINCE_1_0.0
enabled
True if the render-task should be considered for input handling

briefdescription.para: Queries whether the render-task should be considered for input handling. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: True if the render-task should be considered for input handling

briefdescription.para: Sets the actor from which the scene is viewed. @SINCE_1_0.0.

parameterdescription.para: The scene is viewed from the perspective of this actor

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the actor from which the scene is viewed. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: The scene is viewed from the perspective of this actor

briefdescription.para: Sets the frame-buffer used as a render target. @SINCE_1_1.38.

parameterdescription.para: A valid FrameBuffer handle to enable off-screen rendering, or an uninitialized handle to disable it

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the frame-buffer used as a render target. @SINCE_1_1.38.

simplesect.para: The framebuffer

briefdescription.para: Sets the function used to convert screen coordinates to frame-buffer coordinates.

detaileddescription.para:
This is useful for hit-testing actors which are rendered off-screen. @SINCE_1_0.0
conversionFunction
The conversion function

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the function used to convert screen coordinates to frame-buffer coordinates. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: The conversion function

briefdescription.para: Sets the actor used to convert screen coordinates to frame-buffer coordinates.

detaileddescription.para:
The local coordinates of the actor are mapped as frame-buffer coordinates. This is useful for hit-testing actors which are rendered off-screen. @SINCE_1_0.0
mappingActor
The actor used for conversion
The mapping actor needs to be rendered by the default render task to make the mapping work properly.
An Actor can be MappingActor for only one RenderTask.
If input mappingActor is null, then this RenderTask become to do not use mapping Actor.
The mapping actor cannot be used as a Source Actor of any RenderTask.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the actor used to convert screen coordinates to frame-buffer coordinates. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: The actor used for conversion

briefdescription.para: Sets the actor to compute viewport of this render task. Actor should be added on Scene. @SINCE_2_1.36.

parameterdescription.para: This actor is used to compute viewport of the render task.

simplesect.para: If window default camera is rotated and the actor is no longer a rectangle on the screen, Viewport may be computed incorrectly. The Viewport properties VIEWPORT_POSITION and VIEWPORT_SIZE is kept during using ViewportGuideActor, but only current value is changed.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the actor to compute viewport of this render task. @SINCE_2_1.36.

simplesect.para: This actor is used to compute viewport of the render task.

briefdescription.para: Resets the actor to compute viewport of this render task. @SINCE_2_1.36.

simplesect.para: The Viewport properties VIEWPORT_POSITION and VIEWPORT_SIZE is still kept.

briefdescription.para: Sets the scale factor for rendered output.

detaileddescription.para:
The scale factor between source scene and rendered target. For example, if we render 200x200 scene into 50x400 FBO, rendered scale factor is Vector2(0.5f, 2.0f). @SINCE_2_4.36
scaleFactor
The scaling factor to apply to the rendered content

briefdescription.para: Gets the current rendered output scale factor. @SINCE_2_4.36.

simplesect.para: The current scaling factor being applied to rendered content

briefdescription.para: Sets the GL viewport position used when rendering.

detaileddescription.para:
This specifies the transformation between normalized device coordinates and target window (or frame-buffer) coordinates. By default this will match the target window or frame-buffer size. @SINCE_1_0.0
position
The viewports position (x,y)
Unlike the glViewport method, the x & y coordinates refer to the top-left of the viewport rectangle.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the GL viewport position used when rendering. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: The viewport

briefdescription.para: Sets the GL viewport size used when rendering.

detaileddescription.para:
This specifies the transformation between normalized device coordinates and target window (or frame-buffer) coordinates. By default this will match the target window or frame-buffer size. @SINCE_1_0.0
size
The viewports size (width,height)

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the GL viewport size used when rendering. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: The viewport

briefdescription.para: Sets the GL viewport used when rendering.

detaileddescription.para:
This specifies the transformation between normalized device coordinates and target window (or frame-buffer) coordinates. By default this will match the target window or frame-buffer size. @SINCE_1_0.0
viewport
The new viewport
Unlike the glViewport method, the x & y coordinates refer to the top-left of the viewport rectangle.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the GL viewport used when rendering. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: The viewport

briefdescription.para: Sets the clear color used when SetClearEnabled(true) is used. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Retrieves the clear color used when SetClearEnabled(true) is used. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: The clear color
This property can be animated; the return value may not match the value written with SetClearColor().

briefdescription.para: Sets whether the render-task will clear the results of previous render-tasks.

detaileddescription.para: The default is false.

@SINCE_1_0.0
enabled
True if the render-task should clear
The default GL surface is cleared automatically at the beginning of each frame; this setting is only useful when 2+ render-tasks are used, and the result of the first task needs to be (partially) cleared before rendering the second.

briefdescription.para: Queries whether the render-task will clear the results of previous render-tasks. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: True if the render-task should clear

briefdescription.para: Sets whether the render task will cull the actors to the camera's view frustum.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
cullMode
True if the renderers should be culled
The default mode is to cull actors.
If the shader uses Shader::Hint::MODIFIES_GEOMETRY then culling optimizations are disabled.
Shader::Hint

briefdescription.para: Gets the cull mode.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
True if the render task should cull the actors to the camera's view frustum

briefdescription.para: Sets the refresh-rate of the RenderTask.

detaileddescription.para: The default is REFRESH_ALWAYS (1), meaning that the RenderTask will be processed every frame if the scene graph is changing. It may be desirable to process less frequently. For example, SetRefreshRate(3) will process once every 3 frames if the scene graph is changing. If the scene graph is not changing, then the render task will not be rendered, regardless of this value.
The REFRESH_ONCE value means that the RenderTask will be processed once only, to take a snap-shot of the scene. Repeatedly calling SetRefreshRate(REFRESH_ONCE) will cause more snap-shots to be taken.

briefdescription.para: Queries the refresh-rate of the RenderTask. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: The refresh-rate

briefdescription.para: Gets viewport coordinates for given world position.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_1.13
position
The world position
viewportX
The viewport x position
viewportY
The viewport y position
true if the position has a screen coordinate

memberdef.argsstring: (Actor actor, float viewportX, float viewportY, float &localX, float &localY) const
memberdef.name: ViewportToLocal

briefdescription.para: Gets actor local coordinates for given viewport coordinates.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_1.13
actor
The actor describing local coordinate system
viewportX
The viewport x position
viewportY
The viewport y position
localX
The local x position
localY
The local y position
true if the screen position has a local coordinate

Sets Render Pass key for this RenderTask. Shader code that matches this render pass is used for rendering. If no matching shader is found, the code with a render pass of 0 is used. In other cases, operation is not guaranteed.
renderPassTag
RenderPassTag value for this render task.
RenderPassTag of default RenderTask is 0u.

Gets Render Pass key for this RenderTask.
RenderPassTag value for this render task.

Sets Order Index to define rendering order for this RenderTask. In the DALi, offscreen renderTasks are rendered earlier than onscreen renderTask. In each category of OffScreen RenderTask and OnScreen RenderTask, a RenderTask with a smaller orderIndex is rendered first. The RenderTasks in RenderTaskList is always sorted as acending order of the OrderIndex. The OrderIndex value is needed to be set between [-1000, 1000]. Default orderIndex is 0.
orderIndex
the order index for this render task.
The order among RenderTasks whose OrderIndex has not changed follows the order in which they were created.
Rendering order among RenderTasks those have same OrderIndex cannot be guaranteed after the OrderIndex is changed

Gets Order Index for this RenderTask.
OrderIndex value for this render task.

briefdescription.para: Get the unique id of RenderTask. It could be 0 given render task is invalid.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_3.10
The unique id of RenderTask, or 0 if invalid.

briefdescription.para: Stop rendering from given actor. The actor is not included. @SINCE_2_3.23.

parameterdescription.para: A marker to stop rendering. If actor is an empty handle, or stopperActor is not a child of sourceActor, then full scene will be rendered

briefdescription.para: Requests to Keep rendering result of this frame. @SINCE_2_3.51.

simplesect.para: This mothod is only available when the refresh rate is REFRESH_ONCE.
The result can be used after the FinishedSignal is emitted.

briefdescription.para: Requests to Clear the rendered result. @SINCE_2_3.51.

simplesect.para: Since the rendered result is kept in the render thread resource, this method asynchronously clears the result.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the rendered result as PixelData. @SINCE_2_3.51.

simplesect.para: Dali::PixelData that contains rendered result.
This method is only available after the FinishedSignal is emitted. If this method is called before the Finishedsignal is emitted, empty handle is returned.

briefdescription.para: If the refresh rate is REFRESH_ONCE, connect to this signal to be notified when a RenderTask has finished. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: Downcasts a handle to RenderTask handle.

detaileddescription.para:
If handle points to a RenderTask, the downcast produces valid handle. If not, the returned handle is left uninitialized. @SINCE_1_0.0
handle
A handle to an object
A handle to a RenderTask or an uninitialized handle

briefdescription.para: RenderTasks describe how the Dali scene should be rendered.

detaileddescription.para: The Scene provides access to an ordered list of render-tasks.
Each RenderTask must specify the source actors to be rendered, and a camera actor from which the scene is viewed.
RenderTasks may optionally target a frame-buffer, otherwise the default GL surface is used; typically this is a window provided by the native system.

By default Dali provides a single RenderTask, which renders the entire actor hierarchy using a default camera actor and GL surface. If stereoscopic rendering is enabled, Dali will create two additional render tasks, on for each eye. Each render task will have its own camera parented to the default camera actor.
The first RenderTask used for input handling will be the last one rendered, which also has input enabled, and has a valid source & camera actor; see SetInputEnabled().
If none of the actors are hit in the last RenderTask rendered, then input handling will continue with the second last RenderTask rendered, and so on.

All RenderTasks which target a frame-buffer (i.e. off screen) will be rendered before all RenderTasks which target the default GL surface. This allows the user to render intermediate targets which are used later when targeting the screen.
A RenderTask targeting a frame-buffer can still be hit-tested, provided that the screen->frame-buffer coordinate conversion is successful; see SetScreenToFrameBufferFunction().

If the refresh rate id REFRESH_ONCE and a "Finish" signal is connected, it will be emitted when the RenderTask is completed. Note that all connected signals must be disconnected before the object is destroyed. This is typically done in the object destructor, and requires either the Dali::Connection object or Dali::RenderTask handle to be stored.

Signals
Signal Name
Method
finished
FinishedSignal()
@SINCE_1_0.0
